task_claiming.atomicity: GUARANTEED — same-filesystem rename is atomic by POSIX spec
task_claiming.race_loser_handling: FileNotFoundError caught, agent continues to next task
task_claiming.requeue_on_stale: stampede-monitor.sh requeues claimed tasks stale > 10 min via mv

result_writing.atomicity: GUARANTEED — readers see either complete old file or complete new file, never partial

exception_found.description: Orchestrator writes max-generation failure results directly without .tmp- prefix

tmp_prefix_patterns.cleanup_gap: No explicit cleanup of orphaned .tmp- files on agent failure — readers exclude them but files persist

race_conditions.sync_primitive: POSIX rename — sole synchronization mechanism, no locks
race_conditions.design_intent: First mv wins — exactly one agent succeeds, others get FileNotFoundError

toctou_analysis.orphan_requeue: LOW RISK — monitor gated by 10-min stale threshold, isolated writer
toctou_analysis.tmux_session_collision: MINOR — 1s sleep gap between session check and creation (documented)

race_conditions.locking: None — explicitly avoided by design (README.md:215-219, AGENTS.md:68)
landmines_covered: Landmine #2: Race on task claim → atomic mv
Landmine #3: Partial result reads → .tmp- then mv
Landmine #4: Worker dies mid-task → PID detection + requeue
Landmine #17: Branch name collision → stampede/{task_id} unique

append_semantics.o_append_reliance: Explicitly relied upon for shared JSONL ledgers (stampede.sh:674-680)

child_agents_jsonl.format: One JSON object per line + newline

concurrent_write_risk.assessment: MODERATE — O_APPEND makes position atomic but does not guarantee single write(2) syscall for full record
concurrent_write_risk.practical_risk: Low for small JSONL records in practice
concurrent_write_risk.mitigation: Readers explicitly tolerate malformed trailing lines (stampede.sh:680)

append_semantics.per_agent_logs: Mostly isolated per agent (agent-N.log, commander-cli.log) — low contention

overall_assessment.summary: Terminal Stampede implements robust atomic operations throughout. POSIX rename is correctly used as the sole synchronization primitive for task claiming and result writing. The .tmp- prefix pattern is consistently applied and well-documented. Race conditions are handled via FileNotFoundError catch on the claim loop. O_APPEND semantics are explicitly relied upon for shared logs with documented reader tolerance for malformed lines. One minor gap exists: the orchestrator writes max-generation failure results without .tmp- protection (low severity). No orphaned .tmp- cleanup mechanism exists.
gaps: skills/SKILL.md:512-513 — direct write without .tmp- for max-generation failures
No cleanup for orphaned .tmp- files left by crashed agents
Concurrent JSONL writes rely on O_APPEND without full write(2) atomicity guarantee
strengths: POSIX rename used correctly throughout — no locks needed
FileNotFoundError handling is the correct race-loser pattern
Landmine table documents all 24 known failure modes
.tmp- exclusion consistent across all reader paths
O_APPEND reliance is explicit and documented with reader tolerance
